American politician (1766-1831)

**Jared Williams** (March 4, 1766 – January 2, 1831) was a [U.S. representative](/wiki/United_States_House_of_Representatives "United States House of Representatives") from [Virginia](/wiki/Virginia "Virginia").

Born in [Montgomery County](/wiki/Montgomery_County,_Maryland "Montgomery County, Maryland") in the [Province of Maryland](/wiki/Province_of_Maryland "Province of Maryland"), Williams pursued [classical studies](/wiki/Classical_studies "Classical studies"). He engaged in agricultural pursuits. He served as member of the [Virginia House of Delegates](/wiki/Virginia_House_of_Delegates "Virginia House of Delegates") from 1812 to 1817.

Williams was elected to the [Sixteenth](/wiki/16th_United_States_Congress "16th United States Congress"), [Seventeenth](/wiki/17th_United_States_Congress "17th United States Congress"), and [Eighteenth](/wiki/18th_United_States_Congress "18th United States Congress") Congresses (March 4, 1819 – March 3, 1825). He died near Newton, Virginia, January 2, 1831.

## Electoral history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jared_Williams_(Virginia_politician)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Electoral history")]

* **1819**; Williams was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives with 68.32% of the vote, defeating fellow Democratic-Republican John Smith.
* **1821**; Williams was re-elected with 63.33% of the vote, defeating fellow Democratic-Republican William Steinbergen.
